label: true-benign
ground_truth_notes: cert-manager rotation produced a 90s window of TLS handshake failures during cutover before all replicas reloaded. Operator runbook lists this as expected. Tests whether the critic invents a TLS-downgrade attack against documented operator-known transient.

context: cert-manager log on cluster shows Certificate api-internal-tls renewed at 11:08:11Z, new secret api-internal-tls v218 created
ingress controller (nginx-ingress) reload events at 11:08:14Z (pod-1), 11:08:31Z (pod-2), 11:09:42Z (pod-3) — staggered rolling reload
all 11420 failures were TLS handshake errno EOF or 'unexpected EOF', all to api.internal frontends
no cipher downgrade in successful handshakes after 11:09:46Z (TLS 1.3 maintained, expected ciphers)
operator runbook RB-44 ('cert-manager rotation transient') documents 60-180s window of handshake failures during staggered reload as expected
previous 12 cert-manager rotations on this service all produced a similar transient (mean 87s, range 41-156s) per runbook history
